title: Accelerator Catalog
text:
Accelerators are 12-week structured enablement programs delivered by your Customer Success Architect (CSA) , aligned to your mutual success plan and strategic goals. They are included with GitLab’s Advanced (2/year) and Signature (4/year) Success Tiers .

For the full interactive marketplace, visit the GitLab Accelerator Marketplace .

What Is an Accelerator?

An Accelerator is a customized form of enablement that a GitLab CSA defines and delivers in partnership with your team. Each Accelerator:

Runs over approximately 12 weeks

Is scoped to your specific environment, goals, and maturity level

Follows a four-phase lifecycle: Discovery → Design → Deliver → Measure

Produces measurable outcomes tracked in a shared GitLab collaboration project

Customer Success is not permitted to be “hands on keyboard” with customers. CSAs provide best practices, architectural guidance, and tailored adoption plans. If a customer needs GitLab to actively implement or configure their environment, that work is handled by Professional Services .

Customer Success is not permitted to be “hands on keyboard” with customers. CSAs provide best practices, architectural guidance, and tailored adoption plans. If a customer needs GitLab to actively implement or configure their environment, that work is handled by Professional Services .

Accelerator Lifecycle

Assess current state, understand business objectives, and define desired outcomes

Build a customized enablement plan with milestones and a backlog of tracked issues

Execute through workshops, hands-on sessions, and async guidance over ~10 weeks

Accelerator Lifecycle

Assess current state, understand business objectives, and define desired outcomes

Build a customized enablement plan with milestones and a backlog of tracked issues

Execute through workshops, hands-on sessions, and async guidance over ~10 weeks

Assess results against defined outcomes, document impact, and plan the next Accelerator

Available Accelerators

Introduction to CI/CD

Getting-started accelerator covering GitLab CI architecture, gitlab-ci.yml basics, and common pipeline patterns for teams new to the platform

Runner Fleet Guidance

Optimize and manage a GitLab Runner fleet at scale through maturity assessment, informational sessions, and interactive training

Rules and Pipeline Flow

Advanced concepts covering pipeline flow control using rules, DAG, parent/child pipelines, and optimization methods

CI Templates and Reusability

Creation and use of CI templates and workflow optimization for standardized, reusable pipelines

Product Coach (CI/CD)

Deep-dive consultative guidance on pipeline best practices, producing a detailed guidance report across 20+ optimization categories

Pipeline Template Optimization

12-week program developing standardized, reusable templates tailored to your project types, languages, and compliance requirements

Security and Compliance

Intro to Security Workshop

60–90 minute overview of GitLab security concepts, scanners, vulnerability dashboards, and getting-started recommendations

Hands-on Security Lab

Security and Compliance

Intro to Security Workshop

60–90 minute overview of GitLab security concepts, scanners, vulnerability dashboards, and getting-started recommendations

Hands-on Security Lab

90–120 minute interactive lab covering security scanner configuration, security policies, compliance frameworks, and vulnerability triage

Comprehensive 12-week program implementing security scanning, policy configuration, and vulnerability management workflows

Intro to Audit, Compliance, and Separation of Duties

60–90 minute workshop on audit concepts, compliance policies, and best practices for passing audits and enforcing consistency

Security Rollout Strategy

Complete plan for rolling out security and compliance features with timelines, milestones, and a tracked collaboration project backlog

Product Coach (Security)

Deep-dive consultative guidance on security policies, developer workflow, and separation of duties, producing a detailed guidance report

Security Policy Rollout Strategy

Multi-quarter program guiding security policy implementation across five phases: Architecture & Design, Compliance, Audit & Enablement, Dashboards & Integration, and Scaling

Overview of GitLab issue management, backlog workflows, reporting, and planning best practices

Jira + GitLab Workshop

Customized workshop covering integration options between Jira and GitLab, tailored to your current setup and outcomes

AI and Machine Learning

text:
udit & Enablement, Dashboards & Integration, and Scaling

Overview of GitLab issue management, backlog workflows, reporting, and planning best practices

Jira + GitLab Workshop

Customized workshop covering integration options between Jira and GitLab, tailored to your current setup and outcomes

AI and Machine Learning

Bootstrap your developers on all GitLab AI/ML features with hands-on sandbox access

Duo Enterprise Onboarding

12-week program implementing AI-powered development tools across your organization, including customized demonstrations, guided adoption strategies, and champion network development

System Administration

Deep-dive preparation for upcoming GitLab upgrades, including a detailed upgrade plan, deprecation review, and a tracked collaboration project

Managing Monorepos and Large Repositories

Optimization of repository structure, Git LFS implementation, and CI/CD pipeline efficiency for oversized repositories

Optimizing Platform Resiliency

Security hardening, backup strategies, incident response planning, and policy controls to protect your GitLab platform

How to Get an Accelerator

Accelerators are included with GitLab’s Advanced and Signature Success Tiers:

Advanced tier : up to 2 Accelerators per year

Signature tier : up to 4 Accelerators per year

Visit about.gitlab.com/services/ to learn about Success Tiers

Purchase the Advanced or Signature tier

Work with your assigned CSA to select and scope your first Accelerator

For the full interactive catalog, visit the GitLab Accelerator Marketplace .
